title: Break and Continue
description: Learn to stop or skip parts of a running loop.

slides.title: Break 🛑
slides.content:
You already saw the `break` statement used in an earlier chapter to "jump out" of a `switch` statement.

The `break` statement can also be used to jump completely out of a loop!

slides.title: Example
slides.content: This loop is supposed to run 10 times, but we break it early.

quizData.question: What is the LAST number printed in the example above?
quizData.explanation: When i becomes 4, the break statement is triggered, completely terminating the loop. So 3 is the last number printed.

slides.title: Continue ⏭️
slides.content: The `continue` statement breaks one iteration (in the loop) if a specified condition occurs, and continues with the next iteration in the loop.

slides.title: Example
slides.content: Instead of stopping completely, let's just skip the number 4.

quizData.question: What does the code above output?
quizData.explanation: When i is 4, it 'continues' (skips) to the next loop iteration, avoiding the cout statement entirely just for that cycle.
